metadata.title: Build the Black-Scholes Put Pricer with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The eleven-cent gap between the $5.16 model price and the $5.27 quote is really an implied-vol difference, and I want to see it move. I'll extend the pricer to back-solve implied vol with brentq for a whole strike ladder, not just K=170. Help me: (1) plot the implied-vol smile across strikes from the market quotes; (2) compare my flat-vol reprice against the 3pp skew at each strike; (3) show where Jin's protective-put floor cost changes most as the OTM skew steepens.

props.command: The eleven-cent gap between the $5.16 model price and the $5.27 quote is really an implied-vol difference, and I want to see it move. I'll extend the pricer to back-solve implied vol with brentq for a whole strike ladder, not just K=170. Help me: (1) plot the implied-vol smile across strikes from the market quotes; (2) compare my flat-vol reprice against the 3pp skew at each strike; (3) show where Jin's protective-put floor cost changes most as the OTM skew steepens.
props.segment: Build the Black-Scholes Put Pricer with

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: Floor your downside. Price the put.
Build it. Verify it. Break it.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Jin wants to floor his $925K AAPL position for six months. Black-Scholes says the put costs $5.16 per share — but the market quotes $5.27. That eleven-cent gap is the implied volatility difference that professional traders track. The Black-Scholes price is not what the option costs. It is what is consistent with a given volatility assumption.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: $5.16 vs $5.27. The gap is the implied vol premium.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to write the Black-Scholes pricer and back-solver in about forty lines — price the put, then invert for implied vol from the market quote.

props.command: claude "Write a Python function bs_put(S, K, r, T, sigma) that prices a European put via Black-Scholes using scipy.stats.norm.cdf. Add a second function implied_vol(S, K, r, T, market_price) that back-solves sigma using scipy.optimize.brentq. Print both the theoretical price and the implied vol for AAPL: S=185, K=170, r=0.045, T=0.5, market_price=5.27."

beats.narration_text: The code computes d1, d2, then calls norm.cdf with negative arguments for the put formula. The brentq solver brackets implied vol between 1% and 200% and finds the root.

beats.role_note: first output -- Manim animated payoff diagram
beats.narration_text: The put payoff hockey stick draws in — flat at minus the premium above the strike, then rising to the left as the stock falls below $170. The protective put overlaid in gold sets the floor: maximum loss capped at $20.16 per share regardless of how far AAPL falls.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_PutPayoff: hockey-stick put payoff + combined protective put payoff. Floor line annotated. Break-even at $190.16.

beats.narration_text: Now change the volatility assumption: replace flat vol with a simple volatility skew — out-of-the-money puts get sigma plus 3pp, at-the-money stays flat, in-the-money gets sigma minus 3pp.

props.command: claude "Modify bs_put: replace flat sigma=0.25 with a skew: OTM puts (K<S) use sigma+0.03, ATM (K=S±2) use sigma, ITM use sigma-0.03. Recompute the put price for K=170 (OTM) and K=185 (ATM) and K=200 (ITM). Print the skew-adjusted vs flat-vol comparison."

beats.narration_text: The OTM put at K=170 reprices from $5.16 to $5.38 — a 4% jump from adding 3pp of vol skew. The skew makes the implied-vol surface visible: traders pay extra for out-of-the-money protection, the model just maps the relationship.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal table showing flat-vol vs skew-adjusted prices for OTM, ATM, ITM strikes. OTM row highlighted showing the 4% skew premium.

beats.narration_text: The Black-Scholes price is not the option's cost — it is what is consistent with a given volatility. The market price implies the market's volatility estimate. The eleven-cent gap between $5.16 and $5.27 is the market saying its vol estimate is 25.5%, not 25%.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: THE PRICE IS THE VOL. $5.16 at 25%. $5.27 at 25.5%. The model organizes the relationship.

beats.narration_text: Next: try adding a simple volatility skew — OTM puts at sigma plus 3pp, ATM flat. Follow for more computational finance builds.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Add volatility skew: OTM puts cost more. The surface tells the story.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The Black-Scholes price is not the option's cost — it is what is consistent with a given volatility. The market price implies the market's volatility estimate. The eleven-cent gap between $5.16 and $5.27 is the market saying its vol estimate is 25.5%, not 25%. Next: try adding a simple volatility skew — OTM puts at sigma plus 3pp, ATM flat. Follow for more computational finance builds.
